c74object.name: tap.filter~
c74object.description: The tap.filter~ object is a single flexible biquad filter whose type (mode) is selectable at runtime. It implements the full set of Robert Bristow-Johnson "Audio EQ Cookbook" responses — lowpass, highpass, bandpass, bandpass_constant_peak, notch, allpass, peaking, lowshelf, and highshelf — sharing one set of frequency, Q, and gain controls. The cutoff/center frequency can be modulated at audio rate by a signal in the second inlet. For multichannel use, wrap the object in an mc. operator.

inlet.digest: Cutoff / center frequency in Hz (signal-rate modulation or a float)

method.name: clear
method.description: Reset the filter's internal state (flush the delay memory). Useful if the filter blows up.

method.name: signal
method.description: In the left inlet, the audio signal to filter. In the right inlet, a signal drives the cutoff/center frequency for audio-rate modulation.

attribute.name: mode
attribute.description: The filter type. One of: lowpass, highpass, bandpass, bandpass_constant_peak, notch, allpass, peaking, lowshelf, or highshelf. May be changed at runtime.

attribute.name: frequency
attribute.description: The cutoff / center frequency of the filter, in Hz. Defaults to 1000.

attribute.name: q
attribute.description: The Q (resonance / narrowness / steepness) of the filter. For the shelf types this value is reused as the shelf slope S. Defaults to 0.707.

attribute.name: gain
attribute.description: Gain in decibels, applied by the peaking, lowshelf, and highshelf types. Defaults to 0.
